# Kundli AI Agent Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Replace the rule-based 20-field kundli prescription with a tabbed result page where every life-area reading is generated by an AI agent grounded only in Brajesh Gautam's video transcripts, with a chart-aware chat below.

**Architecture:** Astrology compute extended to D-7/D-10/D-12/D-30 + Bhava Chalit + Sayana + KP-lite. New `lib/agent/` module reuses the existing `hybridSearch` + `chat()` plumbing from `/api/ask`. The result page becomes a 9-tab UI with a Style/System toolbar and Astro Wheel SVG visualization. All readings cached by chart hash.

**Tech Stack:** Next.js 14 App Router · TypeScript · `astronomy-engine` · `better-sqlite3` + `sqlite-vec` · OpenRouter (Claude Sonnet 4.5) · Vitest · Tailwind CSS

**Spec:** [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-05-kundli-ai-agent-design.md`](../specs/2026-05-05-kundli-ai-agent-design.md)

## Phase 1: Astrology core extensions

### Task 1.1: Divisional chart helper (generic D-N)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/divisional.ts`
- Test: `tests/astrology/divisional.test.ts`

**Background.** A divisional chart D-N divides each 30° sign into N parts; planet's longitude maps to a new sign. Standard rules per N:

| N | Name | Start sign rule |
|---|---|---|
| 7 | Saptamsa | for odd signs start at same sign; for even signs start at 7th-from-same |
| 9 | Navamsa | (existing — already in compute.ts) |
| 10 | Dasamsa | for odd signs start at same; for even start at 9th-from-same |
| 12 | Dwadasamsa | always starts at the same sign (D-12 of degree d in sign s = (s + floor(d / 2.5)) mod 12) |
| 30 | Trimsamsa | special — odd/even signs use Mars/Saturn/Jupiter/Mercury/Venus in 5-degree segments. Implementation below uses BPHS standard. |

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests** in `tests/astrology/divisional.test.ts`

```ts

import { divisionalSignOf } from '@/lib/astrology/divisional';

describe('divisionalSignOf', () => {
  test('D-9 (Navamsa) of Aries 0° is Aries (0)', () => {
    expect(divisionalSignOf(0, 9)).toBe(0);
  });
  test('D-9 of Taurus 0° (sign 1) starts at Capricorn (9)', () => {
    expect(divisionalSignOf(30, 9)).toBe(9);
  });
  test('D-10 of Aries 0° = Aries (odd start at same)', () => {
    expect(divisionalSignOf(0, 10)).toBe(0);
  });
  test('D-10 of Taurus 0° (even) starts at Capricorn (sign 9)', () => {
    expect(divisionalSignOf(30, 10)).toBe(9);
  });
  test('D-12 of Aries 5° = sign 1 (Taurus)', () => {
    // 5 / 2.5 = 2, 0 + 2 = 2... wait BPHS rule: D-12 always starts at same sign,
    // floor(degInSign / 2.5) units forward. 5/2.5 = 2, so Aries + 2 = Gemini (2)
    expect(divisionalSignOf(5, 12)).toBe(2);
  });
  test('D-7 of odd sign starts at same', () => {
    // Aries 4°: floor(4 * 7 / 30) = 0, Aries + 0 = Aries
    expect(divisionalSignOf(4, 7)).toBe(0);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement** `src/lib/astrology/divisional.ts`

```ts
// Generic divisional chart sign computation per BPHS.
// Returns the sign index (0-11) of a planet's longitude in chart D-N.

export function divisionalSignOf(longitude: number, n: number): number {
  const lon = ((longitude % 360) + 360) % 360;
  const signIdx = Math.floor(lon / 30);
  const degInSign = lon - signIdx * 30;

  switch (n) {
    case 1:
      return signIdx;

    case 7: {
      // Saptamsa: odd signs start at same; even at 7th-from-same.
      const part = Math.floor(degInSign * 7 / 30);
      const start = signIdx % 2 === 0 ? signIdx : (signIdx + 6) % 12;
      return (start + part) % 12;
    }

    case 9: {
      // Navamsa (matches compute.ts navamsaSignOf — kept here for consistency)
      const navIdx = Math.floor(degInSign * 3 / 10);
      let startSign;
      if (signIdx % 3 === 0) startSign = signIdx;
      else if (signIdx % 3 === 1) startSign = (signIdx + 8) % 12;
      else startSign = (signIdx + 4) % 12;
      return (startSign + navIdx) % 12;
    }

    case 10: {
      // Dasamsa: odd signs start at same; even at 9th-from-same.
      const part = Math.floor(degInSign * 10 / 30);
      const start = signIdx % 2 === 0 ? signIdx : (signIdx + 8) % 12;
      return (start + part) % 12;
    }

    case 12: {
      // Dwadasamsa: always starts at same sign, 2.5° per part.
      const part = Math.floor(degInSign / 2.5);
      return (signIdx + part) % 12;
    }

    case 30: {
      // Trimsamsa per BPHS — different rules for odd vs even signs.
      // Odd: Mars(0-5)→Aries, Saturn(5-10)→Aquarius, Jupiter(10-18)→Sagittarius,
      //      Mercury(18-25)→Gemini, Venus(25-30)→Libra
      // Even: Venus(0-5)→Taurus, Mercury(5-12)→Virgo, Jupiter(12-20)→Pisces,
      //       Saturn(20-25)→Capricorn, Mars(25-30)→Scorpio
      const oddTab = [
        { upTo: 5,  sign: 0  },  // Aries
        { upTo: 10, sign: 10 },  // Aquarius
        { upTo: 18, sign: 8  },  // Sagittarius
        { upTo: 25, sign: 2  },  // Gemini
        { upTo: 30, sign: 6  },  // Libra
      ];
      const evenTab = [
        { upTo: 5,  sign: 1  },  // Taurus
        { upTo: 12, sign: 5  },  // Virgo
        { upTo: 20, sign: 11 },  // Pisces
        { upTo: 25, sign: 9  },  // Capricorn
        { upTo: 30, sign: 7  },  // Scorpio
      ];
      const tab = signIdx % 2 === 0 ? oddTab : evenTab;
      for (const row of tab) {
        if (degInSign < row.upTo) return row.sign;
      }
      return tab[tab.length - 1].sign;
    }

    default:
      throw new Error(`Unsupported divisional chart D-${n}`);
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Replace `computeFullChart`** in `compute.ts`

Update import:
```ts
import { divisionalSignOf } from './divisional';
```

Add helper:
```ts
function computeDivisional(d1: Chart, n: number): Chart {
  if (n === 1) return d1;
  const ascSignIdx = divisionalSignOf(d1.ascendant, n);
  const ascDegInSign = d1.ascendant % 30;
  const ascNew = ascSignIdx * 30 + ascDegInSign;
  const planets: PlanetPosition[] = d1.planets.map(p => {
    const sigIdx = divisionalSignOf(p.longitude, n);
    const newLon = sigIdx * 30 + p.degreeInSign;
    return {
      ...p,
      longitude: newLon,
      signIndex: sigIdx,
      sign: SIGNS[sigIdx],
      house: ((sigIdx - ascSignIdx + 12) % 12) + 1,
    };
  });
  return {
    ascendant: ascNew,
    ascSignIndex: ascSignIdx,
    ascSign: SIGNS[ascSignIdx],
    ascDegreeInSign: ascDegInSign,
    planets,
  };
}
```

Replace `computeFullChart`:
```ts
export function computeFullChart(input: BirthInput): FullChart {
  const utc = birthToUtc(input);
  const d1 = computeD1(utc, input.lat, input.lon);
  return {
    input,
    d1,
    d7:  computeDivisional(d1, 7),
    d9:  computeDivisional(d1, 9),
    d10: computeDivisional(d1, 10),
    d12: computeDivisional(d1, 12),
    d30: computeDivisional(d1, 30),
    ayanamsa: getAyanamsa(utc),
    utcDate: utc.toISOString(),
  };
}
```

Remove the now-unused `computeD9` (the divisional helper supersedes it). Update any callers (`api/kundli/route.ts`, `prescription.ts` is being deleted anyway).

### Task 1.3: Vimshottari Dasha

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/dasha.ts`
- Test: `tests/astrology/dasha.test.ts`

**Background.** Vimshottari mahadasha is 120 years total, divided across 9 planets in fixed years: Ketu 7, Venus 20, Sun 6, Moon 10, Mars 7, Rahu 18, Jupiter 16, Saturn 19, Mercury 17. The lord at birth is determined by Moon's nakshatra (each nakshatra has one of the 9 lords cycling). The portion of the first dasha already elapsed at birth = (deg-into-nakshatra / 13°20') × that lord's years.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests**

```ts
import { describe, test, expect } from 'vitest';
import { computeVimshottari, MAHA_YEARS, MAHA_LORDS } from '@/lib/astrology/dasha';

describe('Vimshottari', () => {
  test('120 year cycle sums to 120', () => {
    const total = MAHA_LORDS.reduce((a, l) => a + MAHA_YEARS[l], 0);
    expect(total).toBe(120);
  });

  test('birth at start of Ashwini → Ketu maha for 7 years', () => {
    // Moon at 0° Aries (start of Ashwini, lord = Ketu)
    const birth = new Date('2000-01-01T00:00:00Z');
    const r = computeVimshottari(0, birth);
    expect(r.mahadashas[0].lord).toBe('Ketu');
    const startMs = new Date(r.mahadashas[0].start).getTime();
    const endMs = new Date(r.mahadashas[0].end).getTime();
    const years = (endMs - startMs) / (365.25 * 86400 * 1000);
    expect(years).toBeCloseTo(7, 1);
  });

  test('mid-nakshatra → first maha is shortened', () => {
    // Moon at 6°40' into Ashwini = halfway → 3.5 years remaining of Ketu
    const birth = new Date('2000-01-01T00:00:00Z');
    const r = computeVimshottari(6 + 40/60, birth);
    expect(r.mahadashas[0].lord).toBe('Ketu');
    const years = (new Date(r.mahadashas[0].end).getTime() - new Date(r.mahadashas[0].start).getTime()) / (365.25 * 86400 * 1000);
    expect(years).toBeCloseTo(3.5, 1);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement** `src/lib/astrology/dasha.ts`

```ts
import type { PlanetName, DashaResult, DashaReadout } from './types';

export const MAHA_LORDS: PlanetName[] = ['Ketu','Venus','Sun','Moon','Mars','Rahu','Jupiter','Saturn','Mercury'];
export const MAHA_YEARS: Record<PlanetName, number> = {
  Ketu: 7, Venus: 20, Sun: 6, Moon: 10, Mars: 7, Rahu: 18, Jupiter: 16, Saturn: 19, Mercury: 17,
} as Record<PlanetName, number>;

const YEAR_MS = 365.25 * 86400 * 1000;
const NAKSHATRA_DEG = 360 / 27;  // 13.3333...

function nakshatraIndex(moonLon: number): number {
  const lon = ((moonLon % 360) + 360) % 360;
  return Math.floor(lon / NAKSHATRA_DEG);
}

function lordOfNakshatra(nakIdx: number): PlanetName {
  return MAHA_LORDS[nakIdx % 9];
}

function addYears(t: number, years: number): number {
  return t + years * YEAR_MS;
}

export function computeVimshottari(moonLon: number, birth: Date, now: Date = new Date()): DashaReadout {
  const nakIdx = nakshatraIndex(moonLon);
  const lord0 = lordOfNakshatra(nakIdx);
  const intoNak = ((moonLon % NAKSHATRA_DEG) + NAKSHATRA_DEG) % NAKSHATRA_DEG;
  const fractionElapsed = intoNak / NAKSHATRA_DEG;
  const firstMahaRemaining = MAHA_YEARS[lord0] * (1 - fractionElapsed);

  const mahadashas: DashaResult[] = [];
  let cursorMs = birth.getTime();
  let lordIdx = MAHA_LORDS.indexOf(lord0);
  // first (partial) maha
  const firstEnd = addYears(cursorMs, firstMahaRemaining);
  mahadashas.push({ lord: lord0, start: new Date(cursorMs).toISOString(), end: new Date(firstEnd).toISOString() });
  cursorMs = firstEnd;
  // next 8 full mahas → covers 120-year cycle
  for (let k = 1; k < 9; k++) {
    const lord = MAHA_LORDS[(lordIdx + k) % 9];
    const end = addYears(cursorMs, MAHA_YEARS[lord]);
    mahadashas.push({ lord, start: new Date(cursorMs).toISOString(), end: new Date(end).toISOString() });
    cursorMs = end;
  }

  const nowMs = now.getTime();
  const activeMaha = mahadashas.find(m => nowMs >= new Date(m.start).getTime() && nowMs < new Date(m.end).getTime()) ?? mahadashas[0];

  // Antardasha: 9 sub-periods within active maha, each (sub_lord_years / 120) × maha_total_years
  const mahaStart = new Date(activeMaha.start).getTime();
  const mahaTotalYears = MAHA_YEARS[activeMaha.lord];
  const antardashas: DashaResult[] = [];
  let antarCursor = mahaStart;
  const subLordOrder: PlanetName[] = [];
  const startIdx = MAHA_LORDS.indexOf(activeMaha.lord);
  for (let k = 0; k < 9; k++) subLordOrder.push(MAHA_LORDS[(startIdx + k) % 9]);
  for (const sub of subLordOrder) {
    const yrs = (MAHA_YEARS[sub] * mahaTotalYears) / 120;
    const end = addYears(antarCursor, yrs);
    antardashas.push({ lord: sub, start: new Date(antarCursor).toISOString(), end: new Date(end).toISOString() });
    antarCursor = end;
  }
  const activeAntar = antardashas.find(a => nowMs >= new Date(a.start).getTime() && nowMs < new Date(a.end).getTime()) ?? antardashas[0];

  return { mahadashas, activeMaha, activeAntar, birthMoonNakshatraIndex: nakIdx };
}
```

### Task 1.4: Doshas

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/doshas.ts`
- Test: `tests/astrology/doshas.test.ts`

**Detection rules (V1, conservative):**
- **Mangal Dosh** — Mars in house 1, 4, 7, 8, 12 (counted from D-1 lagna).
- **Kaal Sarp** — All seven non-node planets between Rahu and Ketu (i.e., on one side of the Rahu–Ketu axis).
- **Pitra Dosh** — Sun OR Moon conjunct Rahu/Ketu in same sign, OR malefic in 9th house.
- **Sade Sati** — Saturn currently transiting 12th, 1st, or 2nd from natal Moon sign. (Uses transit at "now".)

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement `doshas.ts`**

```ts
import type { Chart, DoshaResult, PlanetName } from './types';
import { getEclipticLongitude } from './compute'; // export it from compute

export function detectMangal(d1: Chart): DoshaResult {
  const mars = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === 'Mars')!;
  const houses = [1, 4, 7, 8, 12];
  const present = houses.includes(mars.house);
  return { key: 'mangal', present, detail: present ? `Mars in house ${mars.house}` : 'Mars not in 1/4/7/8/12' };
}

export function detectKaalSarp(d1: Chart): DoshaResult {
  const rahu = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === 'Rahu')!.longitude;
  const ketu = (rahu + 180) % 360;
  // Walk arc from Rahu CCW to Ketu — count non-node planets in each half
  function arcContains(start: number, end: number, lon: number): boolean {
    const a = ((lon - start) % 360 + 360) % 360;
    const b = ((end - start) % 360 + 360) % 360;
    return a < b;
  }
  const nonNodes = d1.planets.filter(p => p.name !== 'Rahu' && p.name !== 'Ketu');
  const sideA = nonNodes.filter(p => arcContains(rahu, ketu, p.longitude)).length;
  const sideB = nonNodes.length - sideA;
  const present = sideA === 0 || sideB === 0;
  return { key: 'kaalSarp', present, detail: present ? 'All planets on one side of Rahu-Ketu axis' : 'Planets on both sides' };
}

export function detectPitra(d1: Chart): DoshaResult {
  const planets = d1.planets;
  const sun = planets.find(p => p.name === 'Sun')!;
  const moon = planets.find(p => p.name === 'Moon')!;
  const rahu = planets.find(p => p.name === 'Rahu')!;
  const ketu = planets.find(p => p.name === 'Ketu')!;
  const sunWith = sun.signIndex === rahu.signIndex || sun.signIndex === ketu.signIndex;
  const moonWith = moon.signIndex === rahu.signIndex || moon.signIndex === ketu.signIndex;
  const malefic9 = planets.some(p => ['Saturn','Mars','Rahu','Ketu'].includes(p.name) && p.house === 9);
  const present = sunWith || moonWith || malefic9;
  const reasons = [
    sunWith && 'Sun conjunct Rahu/Ketu',
    moonWith && 'Moon conjunct Rahu/Ketu',
    malefic9 && 'Malefic in 9th',
  ].filter(Boolean).join('; ');
  return { key: 'pitra', present, detail: reasons || 'No Pitra Dosh markers' };
}

export function detectSadeSati(d1: Chart, now: Date = new Date()): DoshaResult {
  // Saturn transit at 'now' relative to natal Moon sign
  const moonSignIdx = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === 'Moon')!.signIndex;
  const satLonRaw = getEclipticLongitude('Saturn', now);
  // apply ayanamsa
  const { getAyanamsa } = require('./compute');
  const satLon = ((satLonRaw - getAyanamsa(now)) % 360 + 360) % 360;
  const satSignIdx = Math.floor(satLon / 30);
  const diff = ((satSignIdx - moonSignIdx + 12) % 12);
  // 12th from Moon = 11, 1st = 0, 2nd = 1
  const present = diff === 11 || diff === 0 || diff === 1;
  return { key: 'sadeSati', present, detail: present ? `Saturn currently in sign ${diff === 11 ? '12th' : diff === 0 ? '1st' : '2nd'} from natal Moon` : 'Saturn not in sade-sati position' };
}

export function detectAllDoshas(d1: Chart, now: Date = new Date()): DoshaResult[] {
  return [detectMangal(d1), detectKaalSarp(d1), detectPitra(d1), detectSadeSati(d1, now)];
}
```

Note: `getEclipticLongitude` and `getAyanamsa` need to be exported from `compute.ts`. Add `export` keyword to both.

### Task 1.5: Yogas

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/yogas.ts`
- Test: `tests/astrology/yogas.test.ts`

**Detection rules:**
- **Raj Yoga** — kendra (1/4/7/10) lord and trikona (1/5/9) lord conjunct, in mutual aspect, or in mutual exchange.
- **Dhana Yoga** — 2nd lord and 11th lord conjunct, OR 5th and 9th lord conjunct.
- **Gajakesari** — Jupiter in kendra (1/4/7/10) from Moon.
- **Pancha-Mahapurusha (5)** — Mars/Mercury/Jupiter/Venus/Saturn in own sign or exalted AND in kendra. Yields Ruchaka/Bhadra/Hamsa/Malavya/Shasha respectively.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests** (synthetic-chart helper + assertions per yoga)
- [ ] **Step 2: Implement `yogas.ts`** following the same style as `doshas.ts`.

```ts
import type { Chart, YogaResult, PlanetName } from './types';
import { lordOfHouse } from './compute';

const KENDRA = [1,4,7,10];
const TRIKONA = [1,5,9];

export function detectRajYogas(d1: Chart): YogaResult[] {
  const out: YogaResult[] = [];
  for (const k of KENDRA) {
    const kLord = lordOfHouse(d1, k);
    const kPos = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === kLord)!;
    for (const t of TRIKONA) {
      if (k === t) continue;
      const tLord = lordOfHouse(d1, t);
      if (tLord === kLord) continue;
      const tPos = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === tLord)!;
      // conjunction: same sign
      if (kPos.signIndex === tPos.signIndex) {
        out.push({
          key: `raj-${k}-${t}`, name: 'Raj Yoga', planets: [kLord, tLord],
          detail: `Lords of houses ${k} and ${t} (${kLord}, ${tLord}) conjunct in ${kPos.sign}`,
        });
      }
    }
  }
  return out;
}

export function detectDhanaYogas(d1: Chart): YogaResult[] {
  const pairs = [[2,11], [5,9]];
  const out: YogaResult[] = [];
  for (const [a, b] of pairs) {
    const la = lordOfHouse(d1, a), lb = lordOfHouse(d1, b);
    if (la === lb) continue;
    const pa = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === la)!;
    const pb = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === lb)!;
    if (pa.signIndex === pb.signIndex) {
      out.push({
        key: `dhana-${a}-${b}`, name: 'Dhana Yoga', planets: [la, lb],
        detail: `Lords of ${a} and ${b} (${la}, ${lb}) conjunct in ${pa.sign}`,
      });
    }
  }
  return out;
}

export function detectGajakesari(d1: Chart): YogaResult[] {
  const moon = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === 'Moon')!;
  const jup = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === 'Jupiter')!;
  const diff = ((jup.signIndex - moon.signIndex + 12) % 12) + 1;
  if (KENDRA.includes(diff)) {
    return [{
      key: 'gajakesari', name: 'Gajakesari Yoga', planets: ['Jupiter','Moon'],
      detail: `Jupiter in ${diff}th from Moon`,
    }];
  }
  return [];
}

const OWN_SIGNS: Record<PlanetName, number[]> = {
  Sun: [4], Moon: [3], Mars: [0,7], Mercury: [2,5], Jupiter: [8,11], Venus: [1,6], Saturn: [9,10],
  Rahu: [], Ketu: [],
};
const EXALTATION: Record<PlanetName, number> = {
  Sun: 0, Moon: 1, Mars: 9, Mercury: 5, Jupiter: 3, Venus: 11, Saturn: 6, Rahu: -1, Ketu: -1,
};
const PMP_NAMES: Record<string, string> = {
  Mars: 'Ruchaka', Mercury: 'Bhadra', Jupiter: 'Hamsa', Venus: 'Malavya', Saturn: 'Shasha',
};

export function detectPanchaMahapurusha(d1: Chart): YogaResult[] {
  const out: YogaResult[] = [];
  for (const name of Object.keys(PMP_NAMES) as PlanetName[]) {
    const pos = d1.planets.find(p => p.name === name)!;
    const inOwn = OWN_SIGNS[name].includes(pos.signIndex);
    const exalted = EXALTATION[name] === pos.signIndex;
    const inKendra = KENDRA.includes(pos.house);
    if ((inOwn || exalted) && inKendra) {
      out.push({
        key: `pmp-${name.toLowerCase()}`,
        name: `${PMP_NAMES[name]} Yoga`,
        planets: [name],
        detail: `${name} ${exalted ? 'exalted' : 'in own sign'} (${pos.sign}) in house ${pos.house} (kendra)`,
      });
    }
  }
  return out;
}

export function detectAllYogas(d1: Chart): YogaResult[] {
  return [
    ...detectRajYogas(d1),
    ...detectDhanaYogas(d1),
    ...detectGajakesari(d1),
    ...detectPanchaMahapurusha(d1),
  ];
}
```

### Task 1.7: Bhava Chalit (Sripati cusps)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/bhavaChalit.ts`
- Modify: `src/lib/astrology/compute.ts`

**Sripati cusps:** Lagna = cusp of house 1. Cusp of house 4 = Imum Coeli (IC, 90° from MC). Cusp of house 7 = Descendant. Cusp of house 10 = MC. Intermediate cusps interpolate equally between angular cusps. Each planet's house = the cusp range it falls in.

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement** `src/lib/astrology/bhavaChalit.ts`

```ts
import type { Chart, PlanetPosition } from './types';
import { SIGNS } from './types';
import * as Astronomy from 'astronomy-engine';

function mc(date: Date, lonEast: number): number {
  const time = Astronomy.MakeTime(date);
  const gst = Astronomy.SiderealTime(time);
  const lstDeg = ((gst * 15 + lonEast) % 360 + 360) % 360;
  // Tropical MC = LST projected on ecliptic
  const epsRad = 23.4367 * Math.PI / 180;
  const ramcRad = lstDeg * Math.PI / 180;
  const mcRad = Math.atan2(Math.sin(ramcRad), Math.cos(ramcRad) * Math.cos(epsRad));
  let mcDeg = mcRad * 180 / Math.PI;
  if (mcDeg < 0) mcDeg += 360;
  return mcDeg;
}

export function computeBhavaChalit(d1: Chart, date: Date, lonEast: number, ayanamsa: number): Chart {
  const asc = d1.ascendant;                              // sidereal
  const mcSidereal = ((mc(date, lonEast) - ayanamsa) % 360 + 360) % 360;
  const ic = (mcSidereal + 180) % 360;
  const desc = (asc + 180) % 360;

  const cusps = new Array<number>(12);
  cusps[0] = asc;
  cusps[3] = ic;
  cusps[6] = desc;
  cusps[9] = mcSidereal;
  function span(a: number, b: number) { return ((b - a) % 360 + 360) % 360; }
  function interp(start: number, end: number) {
    const s = span(start, end);
    return [(start + s/3) % 360, (start + 2*s/3) % 360];
  }
  [cusps[1], cusps[2]]   = interp(asc, ic);
  [cusps[4], cusps[5]]   = interp(ic, desc);
  [cusps[7], cusps[8]]   = interp(desc, mcSidereal);
  [cusps[10], cusps[11]] = interp(mcSidereal, asc);

  function houseOf(lon: number): number {
    for (let h = 0; h < 12; h++) {
      const a = cusps[h], b = cusps[(h+1) % 12];
      const offset = span(a, lon);
      const range = span(a, b);
      if (offset < range) return h + 1;
    }
    return 1;
  }

  const planets: PlanetPosition[] = d1.planets.map(p => ({ ...p, house: houseOf(p.longitude) }));
  return {
    ...d1,
    planets,
  };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Wire into `computeFullChart`** — needs UTC date and lon:

```ts

return { ...prev, bhavaChalit };
```

## Phase 2: KP module

### Task 2.1: KP sub-lord table

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/astrology/kp.ts`
- Test: `tests/astrology/kp.test.ts`

**Background.** The 360° zodiac is divided into 27 nakshatras × 9 sub-lords each = 249 segments (27×9 but boundaries within each nakshatra use Vimshottari proportions, so effectively 249 unique sub-lord boundaries). Each segment is owned by a {nakshatra-lord, sub-lord} pair. The sub-lord of a longitude is found by walking forward from the nakshatra start in Vimshottari proportions (Ketu 7, Venus 20, …) until the longitude is enclosed.

- [ ] **Step 1: Build the segment table once**

```ts
import { MAHA_LORDS, MAHA_YEARS } from './dasha';
import type { PlanetName } from './types';

const NAKSHATRA_DEG = 360 / 27;

export interface SubLordSegment { startLon: number; endLon: number; star: PlanetName; sub: PlanetName; }
const SEGMENTS: SubLordSegment[] = (() => {
  const out: SubLordSegment[] = [];
  for (let n = 0; n < 27; n++) {
    const star = MAHA_LORDS[n % 9];
    const nakStart = n * NAKSHATRA_DEG;
    let cursor = nakStart;
    const subOrder: PlanetName[] = [];
    const startIdx = MAHA_LORDS.indexOf(star);
    for (let k = 0; k < 9; k++) subOrder.push(MAHA_LORDS[(startIdx + k) % 9]);
    for (const sub of subOrder) {
      const fraction = MAHA_YEARS[sub] / 120;
      const segLen = NAKSHATRA_DEG * fraction;
      out.push({ startLon: cursor, endLon: cursor + segLen, star, sub });
      cursor += segLen;
    }
  }
  return out;
})();

export function subLordOf(longitude: number): { star: PlanetName; sub: PlanetName; subSub: PlanetName } {
  const lon = ((longitude % 360) + 360) % 360;
  const seg = SEGMENTS.find(s => lon >= s.startLon && lon < s.endLon)!;
  // sub-sub: divide seg by Vimshottari proportions starting from sub
  const subStartIdx = MAHA_LORDS.indexOf(seg.sub);
  const segLen = seg.endLon - seg.startLon;
  let cursor = seg.startLon;
  for (let k = 0; k < 9; k++) {
    const subSub = MAHA_LORDS[(subStartIdx + k) % 9];
    const fraction = MAHA_YEARS[subSub] / 120;
    const ssLen = segLen * fraction;
    if (lon < cursor + ssLen) return { star: seg.star, sub: seg.sub, subSub };
    cursor += ssLen;
  }
  return { star: seg.star, sub: seg.sub, subSub: seg.sub };
}
```

```ts
test('249 sub-lord segments tile 360°', () => {

  for (let i = 0; i < 3600; i++) {
    const lon = i * 0.1;
    const r = subLordOf(lon);
    expect(r.star).toBeDefined();
    expect(r.sub).toBeDefined();

});

test('sub-lord at 0° = Ketu/Ketu', () => {
  const r = subLordOf(0.001);  // just inside first segment
  expect(r.star).toBe('Ketu');
  expect(r.sub).toBe('Ketu');
});
```

### Task 2.2: Placidus cusps + KP readout

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/lib/astrology/kp.ts`
- Modify: `src/lib/astrology/compute.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement Placidus** in `kp.ts`

```ts
import * as Astronomy from 'astronomy-engine';

function placidusCusps(date: Date, lat: number, lonEast: number, ayanamsa: number): number[] {
  const time = Astronomy.MakeTime(date);
  const gst = Astronomy.SiderealTime(time);
  const lstDeg = ((gst * 15 + lonEast) % 360 + 360) % 360;
  const epsRad = 23.4367 * Math.PI / 180;
  const phiRad = lat * Math.PI / 180;

  // MC tropical
  const mcRad = Math.atan2(Math.sin(lstDeg * Math.PI / 180), Math.cos(lstDeg * Math.PI / 180) * Math.cos(epsRad));
  let mcDeg = ((mcRad * 180 / Math.PI) % 360 + 360) % 360;

  // Ascendant tropical (recompute, same as compute.ts)
  const ramcRad = lstDeg * Math.PI / 180;
  const y = Math.cos(ramcRad);
  const x = -(Math.sin(ramcRad) * Math.cos(epsRad) + Math.tan(phiRad) * Math.sin(epsRad));
  let asc = Math.atan2(y, x) * 180 / Math.PI;
  if (asc < 0) asc += 360;
  const diff = ((asc - lstDeg) % 360 + 360) % 360;
  if (diff > 180) asc = (asc + 180) % 360;

  // Intermediate cusps via Placidus formula (semi-arc method)
  function intermediate(housePos: number /* 11, 12, 2, 3 */): number {
    // Approximation per Wikipedia "Placidus" — iterative.
    const fraction = housePos === 11 ? 1/3 : housePos === 12 ? 2/3 : housePos === 2 ? 1/3 : 2/3;
    const RA = (housePos === 11 || housePos === 12) ? lstDeg + 30 * (housePos - 10) : lstDeg + 30 * (housePos + 2);
    let lambda = RA;
    for (let it = 0; it < 10; it++) {
      const lambdaRad = lambda * Math.PI / 180;
      const decl = Math.asin(Math.sin(lambdaRad) * Math.sin(epsRad));
      const f = Math.tan(phiRad) * Math.tan(decl);
      const H = Math.acos(-f * (1 - 2 * fraction));
      const newRA = (RA - H * 180 / Math.PI + 360) % 360;
      lambda = newRA;
    }
    const lr = lambda * Math.PI / 180;
    let ec = Math.atan2(Math.sin(lr) * Math.cos(epsRad) - Math.tan(0) * Math.sin(epsRad), Math.cos(lr));
    let ecDeg = ec * 180 / Math.PI;
    if (ecDeg < 0) ecDeg += 360;
    return ecDeg;
  }

  const cuspsTrop = new Array<number>(12);
  cuspsTrop[0] = asc;
  cuspsTrop[9] = mcDeg;
  cuspsTrop[6] = (asc + 180) % 360;
  cuspsTrop[3] = (mcDeg + 180) % 360;
  cuspsTrop[10] = intermediate(11);
  cuspsTrop[11] = intermediate(12);
  cuspsTrop[1]  = intermediate(2);
  cuspsTrop[2]  = intermediate(3);
  cuspsTrop[4]  = (cuspsTrop[10] + 180) % 360;
  cuspsTrop[5]  = (cuspsTrop[11] + 180) % 360;
  cuspsTrop[7]  = (cuspsTrop[1]  + 180) % 360;
  cuspsTrop[8]  = (cuspsTrop[2]  + 180) % 360;

  // Apply Lahiri ayanamsa to convert to sidereal
  return cuspsTrop.map(c => ((c - ayanamsa) % 360 + 360) % 360);
}

export function computeKP(d1: Chart, date: Date, lat: number, lonEast: number, ayanamsa: number): KPReadout {
  const cusps = placidusCusps(date, lat, lonEast, ayanamsa);
  const cuspSubLords = cusps.map(c => subLordOf(c).sub);
  const planetSubLords = {} as Record<PlanetName, { star: PlanetName; sub: PlanetName; subSub: PlanetName }>;
  for (const p of d1.planets) {
    planetSubLords[p.name] = subLordOf(p.longitude);
  }
  return { cusps, cuspSubLords, planetSubLords };
}
```

Add the import `Chart, KPReadout, PlanetName` from `./types` at top of `kp.ts`.

```ts
return { ...prev, kp };
```

## Phase 4: AI agent

### Task 4.1: Chart context serializer

**Files:**
- Create: `src/lib/agent/chartContext.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement** — turn FullChart into compact text fed to LLM. Per-tab variants:

```ts
import type { FullChart, Chart, PlanetName } from '@/lib/astrology/types';

export type TabKey = 'overview' | 'marriage' | 'career' | 'children' | 'parents' | 'hardships' | 'doshas-yogas' | 'dashas' | 'compare';

function chartFacts(c: Chart, label: string): string {
  const head = `${label} — Lagna: ${c.ascSign} ${c.ascDegreeInSign.toFixed(1)}°.`;
  const planets = c.planets.map(p => `${p.name} ${p.sign} ${p.degreeInSign.toFixed(1)}° (house ${p.house}, ${p.nakshatra} pada ${p.pada}${p.retrograde ? ', R' : ''})`).join('; ');
  return `${head}\n  ${planets}`;
}

export function chartContextFor(tab: TabKey, fc: FullChart): string {
  const lines: string[] = [];
  lines.push(`BIRTH: ${fc.input.placeName} ${fc.utcDate} (UTC). Ayanamsa ${fc.ayanamsa.toFixed(2)}°.`);
  lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d1, 'D-1 Lagna'));
  switch (tab) {
    case 'marriage':
      lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d9, 'D-9 Navamsa'));
      break;
    case 'career':
      lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d10, 'D-10 Dasamsa'));
      break;
    case 'children':
      lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d7, 'D-7 Saptamsa'));
      break;
    case 'parents':
      lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d12, 'D-12 Dwadasamsa'));
      break;
    case 'hardships':
      lines.push(chartFacts(fc.d30, 'D-30 Trimsamsa'));
      break;
    case 'overview':
    case 'doshas-yogas':
    case 'dashas':
    case 'compare':
      // overview just uses d1
      break;
  }
  if (fc.doshas?.length) {
    const present = fc.doshas.filter(d => d.present).map(d => `${d.key}: ${d.detail}`).join('; ');
    if (present) lines.push(`DOSHAS PRESENT: ${present}`);
  }
  if (fc.yogas?.length) {
    lines.push(`YOGAS: ${fc.yogas.map(y => `${y.name} (${y.detail})`).join('; ')}`);
  }
  if (fc.dasha) {
    lines.push(`ACTIVE DASHA: ${fc.dasha.activeMaha.lord} maha / ${fc.dasha.activeAntar.lord} antar (until ${fc.dasha.activeAntar.end.slice(0,10)})`);
  }
  return lines.join('\n');
}

const TAB_PROBES: Record<TabKey, (fc: FullChart) => string> = {
  overview: fc => `lagna ${fc.d1.ascSign}, ${fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Moon')!.sign} moon, life path`,
  marriage: fc => {
    const v = fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Venus')!;
    const seventhSign = (fc.d1.ascSignIndex + 6) % 12;
    return `marriage spouse navamsa Venus ${v.sign} 7th house mangal dosh`;
  },
  career: fc => {
    const sun = fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Sun')!;
    const sat = fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Saturn')!;
    return `career profession dasamsa Sun ${sun.sign} Saturn ${sat.sign} 10th house`;
  },
  children: fc => {
    const jup = fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Jupiter')!;
    return `children santaan saptamsa Jupiter ${jup.sign} 5th house`;
  },
  parents: fc => `parents mata pita 4th 9th house dwadasamsa father mother`,
  hardships: fc => {
    const sat = fc.d1.planets.find(p=>p.name==='Saturn')!;
    return `hardships dukh trimsamsa Saturn ${sat.sign} 6 8 12 dosha`;
  },
  'doshas-yogas': fc => {
    const dlist = (fc.doshas ?? []).filter(d=>d.present).map(d=>d.key).join(' ');
    const ylist = (fc.yogas ?? []).map(y=>y.name).join(' ');
    return `${dlist} ${ylist} dosha yoga remedy`;
  },
  dashas: fc => `${fc.dasha?.activeMaha.lord} mahadasha ${fc.dasha?.activeAntar.lord} antardasha period`,
  compare: () => `sidereal tropical KP comparison`,
};

export function probeQueryFor(tab: TabKey, fc: FullChart): string {
  return TAB_PROBES[tab](fc);
}
```

```ts
export const READING_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are the editor of "Anchor of Life" — a digital archive of Brajesh Gautam ji's recorded teachings. The user has just generated their kundli (birth chart). Your job is to compose a reading for ONE specific life area, grounded EXCLUSIVELY in the BG transcript excerpts provided.

GOLDEN RULES:
1. Use ONLY the provided transcripts. If transcripts don't cover a chart feature, acknowledge the gap honestly. Never invent BG quotes or fill in with generic Vedic astrology.
2. The chart facts (planets, houses, doshas, yogas, dasha) are the lens. The transcripts are the source. Connect chart facts to what BG actually said.
3. Cite every claim with {{cite:N}} markers. The N matches a Source from the user message.
4. Match the user's language (en or hi). Hindi answers in Devanagari.

STRUCTURE — three paragraphs:

[1] WHAT THE CHART SHOWS (2–3 sentences) — Plain-language statement of the most important chart feature for THIS life area, e.g., "Your D-9 lagna is Pisces with Jupiter in 7th house — this is the marriage canvas." Reference 1–2 specific chart facts. Cite if BG describes the feature: {{cite:1}}.

[2] WHAT BRAJESH JI TEACHES ABOUT THIS (4–6 sentences) — The core teaching from the transcripts on this configuration. Quote his framing, his metaphors, his reasoning. Cite each major point: {{cite:N}}. If transcripts don't directly cover this configuration but cover an adjacent one, say so: "Brajesh ji speaks of similar Venus configurations in this teaching {{cite:2}}."

[3] WHAT TO HOLD (2–4 sentences) — A practical takeaway, but ONLY if BG actually says one in the cited sources. If not, end with: "For a personalized remedy and timing, ब्रजेश जी invite a personal consultation." Never fabricate remedies.

VOICE:
- Write *about* Brajesh ji's teaching ("Brajesh ji teaches…", "He explains that…"). Do not write *as* him.
- Calm, direct, slightly literary. Sentences are statements, not preludes.
- No "amazing", "incredible", emojis, marketing tone.

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON):
{
  "answer_md": "3-paragraph reading with {{cite:N}} markers",
  "primary_citation_index": 1,
  "lens": { "inner": bool, "jyotish": true, "practice": bool },
  "related_topics": ["string", ...],
  "language": "en" | "hi"
}`;

export const CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are the editor of "Anchor of Life" answering a question about the user's kundli, grounded EXCLUSIVELY in Brajesh Gautam ji's transcript excerpts.

The user's CHART CONTEXT will be in the user message — treat it as context the user has shared with you, not as a transcript source.

GOLDEN RULES:
1. Source claims ONLY from the provided transcript excerpts. If a chart feature isn't covered, say so.
2. Connect the user's specific chart facts to what BG taught.
3. Cite every claim with {{cite:N}}.
4. Match the user's language (en/hi).

STRUCTURE: a single focused answer (2–4 paragraphs, 200–400 words). Lead with the direct answer. Expand with BG's teaching. Close with what to hold or "for personalized guidance, ब्रजेश जी invite a personal consultation."

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON):
{
  "answer_md": "answer with {{cite:N}} markers",
  "primary_citation_index": 1,
  "lens": { "inner": bool, "jyotish": bool, "practice": bool },
  "related_topics": ["string", ...],
  "language": "en" | "hi"
}`;
```

```ts
import { hybridSearch, buildCitationsFromVideos } from '@/lib/search';
import { chat } from '@/lib/openrouter';
import type { AnswerEnvelope, Lang } from '@/lib/types';
import type { FullChart } from '@/lib/astrology/types';
import { chartContextFor, probeQueryFor, type TabKey } from './chartContext';
import { READING_SYSTEM_PROMPT } from './prompts';

function fmtTs(s: number) { const m = Math.floor(s / 60); const sec = Math.floor(s % 60); return `${m}:${String(sec).padStart(2,'0')}`; }

export async function generateReading(tab: TabKey, fc: FullChart, language: Lang): Promise<AnswerEnvelope> {
  const probe = probeQueryFor(tab, fc);
  const result = await hybridSearch(probe, 4);
  const citations = buildCitationsFromVideos(result);
  const ctx = chartContextFor(tab, fc);

  const sources = citations.map((c, i) =>
    `[Source ${i + 1}] "${c.title}" — at ${fmtTs(c.start_sec)}\n${c.quote}`
  ).join('\n\n---\n\n');

  const userPrompt = `LIFE AREA: ${tab}
LANGUAGE: ${language}

USER CHART CONTEXT:
${ctx}

BG TRANSCRIPT SOURCES (ranked by relevance):
${sources}

Compose the 3-paragraph reading per the system instructions. Aim for 220-380 words. Cite all 4 sources where they support a claim. Return ONLY the JSON object.

Language for the answer: ${language === 'hi' ? 'Hindi (Devanagari script)' : 'English'}.`;

  const raw = await chat(
    [{ role: 'system', content: READING_SYSTEM_PROMPT }, { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }],
    { temperature: 0.3, max_tokens: 1500, response_format: { type: 'json_object' } }
  );

  let parsed: any;
  try { parsed = JSON.parse(raw); }
  catch {
    const m = raw.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
    parsed = m ? JSON.parse(m[0]) : {};
  }
  return {
    answer_md: String(parsed.answer_md ?? ''),
    primary_citation_index: Math.max(1, Math.min(citations.length, parseInt(parsed.primary_citation_index) || 1)),
    citations,
    lens: { inner: !!parsed.lens?.inner, jyotish: !!parsed.lens?.jyotish, practice: !!parsed.lens?.practice },
    related_topics: Array.isArray(parsed.related_topics) ? parsed.related_topics.slice(0, 5) : [],
    language,
    total_mentions: 0,
    composer_model: process.env.COMPOSER_MODEL ?? 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5',
  };
}
```

### Task 4.3: /api/kundli/reading endpoint

**Files:**
- Create: `src/app/api/kundli/reading/route.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement**

```ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { z } from 'zod';
import { computeFullChart } from '@/lib/astrology/compute';
import { generateReading } from '@/lib/agent/reading';
import type { TabKey } from '@/lib/agent/chartContext';
import type { BirthInput } from '@/lib/astrology/types';

export const runtime = 'nodejs';
export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';

const Body = z.object({
  input: z.object({
    date: z.string(),
    time: z.string(),
    tzOffsetMinutes: z.number(),
    lat: z.number(),
    lon: z.number(),
    placeName: z.string(),
    name: z.string().optional(),
  }),
  tab: z.enum(['overview','marriage','career','children','parents','hardships','doshas-yogas','dashas','compare']),
  language: z.enum(['en','hi']).default('en'),
});

const CACHE: Record<string, { at: number; data: any }> = {};
const TTL = 24 * 3600 * 1000;

function cacheKey(input: BirthInput, tab: TabKey, lang: string): string {
  const s = `${input.date}|${input.time}|${input.tzOffsetMinutes}|${input.lat}|${input.lon}|${tab}|${lang}`;
  let h = 0; for (let i = 0; i < s.length; i++) h = ((h << 5) - h + s.charCodeAt(i)) | 0;
  return String(h);
}

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  let body;
  try { body = Body.parse(await req.json()); }
  catch (e: any) { return NextResponse.json({ error: 'invalid_request', detail: e.message }, { status: 400 }); }

  const ck = cacheKey(body.input as BirthInput, body.tab as TabKey, body.language);
  const c = CACHE[ck];
  if (c && Date.now() - c.at < TTL) return NextResponse.json(c.data, { headers: { 'X-Cache': 'HIT' } });

  const fc = computeFullChart(body.input as BirthInput);
  const envelope = await generateReading(body.tab as TabKey, fc, body.language);
  CACHE[ck] = { at: Date.now(), data: envelope };
  return NextResponse.json(envelope, { headers: { 'X-Cache': 'MISS' } });
}
```

```ts
import { hybridSearch, buildCitationsFromVideos } from '@/lib/search';
import { chat as openrouterChat } from '@/lib/openrouter';
import type { AnswerEnvelope, Lang } from '@/lib/types';
import type { FullChart } from '@/lib/astrology/types';
import { chartContextFor } from './chartContext';
import { CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT } from './prompts';

interface ChatTurn { role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string }

function fmtTs(s: number) { const m = Math.floor(s / 60); const sec = Math.floor(s % 60); return `${m}:${String(sec).padStart(2,'0')}`; }

export async function generateChatReply(
  fc: FullChart, history: ChatTurn[], userMessage: string, language: Lang
): Promise<AnswerEnvelope> {
  const result = await hybridSearch(userMessage, 4);
  const citations = buildCitationsFromVideos(result);
  const ctx = chartContextFor('overview', fc); // overview context = full d1 + doshas/yogas/dasha
  const sources = citations.map((c, i) =>
    `[Source ${i + 1}] "${c.title}" at ${fmtTs(c.start_sec)}\n${c.quote}`
  ).join('\n\n---\n\n');

  const messages = [
    { role: 'system' as const, content: CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT },
    ...history.slice(-6).map(t => ({ role: t.role as 'user'|'assistant', content: t.content })),
    { role: 'user' as const, content: `USER CHART:\n${ctx}\n\nUSER QUESTION (lang ${language}):\n${userMessage}\n\nBG TRANSCRIPT SOURCES:\n${sources}\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON.` },
  ];

  const raw = await openrouterChat(messages, { temperature: 0.4, max_tokens: 1500, response_format: { type: 'json_object' } });
  let parsed: any;
  try { parsed = JSON.parse(raw); } catch { const m = raw.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/); parsed = m ? JSON.parse(m[0]) : {}; }
  return {
    answer_md: String(parsed.answer_md ?? ''),
    primary_citation_index: Math.max(1, Math.min(citations.length, parseInt(parsed.primary_citation_index) || 1)),
    citations,
    lens: { inner: !!parsed.lens?.inner, jyotish: !!parsed.lens?.jyotish, practice: !!parsed.lens?.practice },
    related_topics: Array.isArray(parsed.related_topics) ? parsed.related_topics.slice(0,5) : [],
    language,
    total_mentions: 0,
    composer_model: process.env.COMPOSER_MODEL ?? 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5',
  };
}
```

### Task 5.3: AstroWheel SVG component

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/kundli/AstroWheel.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement** (~150 lines, SVG with concentric rings)

Render: outer ring with 12 zodiac signs (30° each), inner ring with 12 house cusps (Whole-sign or KP cusps), planet glyphs at their longitude. Lagna marked with a pointer. Use `SIGN_GLYPHS` from `types.ts`.

```tsx
'use client';
import type { Chart } from '@/lib/astrology/types';
import { PLANETS, SIGN_GLYPHS } from '@/lib/astrology/types';

interface Props { chart: Chart; size?: number; cusps?: number[]; }

export default function AstroWheel({ chart, size = 380, cusps }: Props) {
  const cx = size / 2, cy = size / 2;
  const rOuter = size * 0.46;
  const rInner = size * 0.28;
  const ascDeg = chart.ascendant;
  // SVG: 0° at left (East/Asc), going CCW for traditional Western feel,
  // OR 0° at top, going CW for South-style. We'll go 0° at left, CCW.
  const toXY = (lon: number, r: number) => {
    // angle from Asc going CCW; rotate so Asc sits on left.
    const angleDeg = (lon - ascDeg + 360) % 360;
    const angleRad = (180 - angleDeg) * Math.PI / 180;
    return { x: cx + r * Math.cos(angleRad), y: cy - r * Math.sin(angleRad) };
  };

  const houseLines = (cusps ?? Array.from({length: 12}, (_, i) => (chart.ascSignIndex + i) * 30 + chart.ascDegreeInSign)).map((c, i) => {
    const p1 = toXY(c, rInner);
    const p2 = toXY(c, rOuter);
    return <line key={i} x1={p1.x} y1={p1.y} x2={p2.x} y2={p2.y} stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth={i % 3 === 0 ? 1.5 : 0.5} opacity={0.7}/>;
  });

  const signLabels = Array.from({length: 12}, (_, i) => {
    const lon = i * 30 + 15;
    const p = toXY(lon, (rInner + rOuter) / 2);
    return <text key={i} x={p.x} y={p.y} textAnchor="middle" dominantBaseline="middle" fontSize={size * 0.04} fill="currentColor">{SIGN_GLYPHS[i]}</text>;
  });

  const planetGlyphs = chart.planets.map((p, i) => {
    const r = rInner * (0.6 + (i % 3) * 0.1);
    const xy = toXY(p.longitude, r);
    return (
      <g key={p.name}>
        <text x={xy.x} y={xy.y} textAnchor="middle" dominantBaseline="middle" fontSize={size * 0.045} fill="currentColor">{p.symbol}</text>
        <text x={xy.x} y={xy.y + size*0.04} textAnchor="middle" fontSize={size * 0.025} fill="currentColor" opacity={0.6}>{p.degreeInSign.toFixed(0)}°</text>
      </g>
    );
  });

  return (
    <svg viewBox={`0 0 ${size} ${size}`} className="text-ink" width={size} height={size}>
      <circle cx={cx} cy={cy} r={rOuter} fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth={1}/>
      <circle cx={cx} cy={cy} r={rInner} fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth={0.8} opacity={0.6}/>
      <circle cx={cx} cy={cy} r={size * 0.04} fill="currentColor" opacity={0.1}/>
      {houseLines}
      {signLabels}
      {planetGlyphs}
      {/* Asc pointer on left */}
      <text x={cx - rOuter - 8} y={cy + 4} textAnchor="end" fontSize={size * 0.03} fill="currentColor" opacity={0.7}>Asc</text>
    </svg>
  );
}
```

### Task 5.4: Reading + ChartChat components

**Files:**

- Create: `src/components/kundli/ChartChat.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: `Reading.tsx`** — accepts `tab`, `chartInput`, `lang`. Calls `/api/kundli/reading` on mount, shows loading skeleton, renders `answer_md` with `{{cite:N}}` replaced by clickable citation chips that open the BG video at the timestamp.

```tsx
'use client';
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import type { Lang } from '@/lib/types';
import type { TabKey } from '@/lib/agent/chartContext';
import type { BirthInput } from '@/lib/astrology/types';
import type { AnswerEnvelope } from '@/lib/types';

export default function Reading({ tab, input, lang }: { tab: TabKey; input: BirthInput; lang: Lang }) {
  const [data, setData] = useState<AnswerEnvelope | null>(null);
  const [err, setErr] = useState<string | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    setData(null);
    setErr(null);
    const cacheKey = `kundli-reading:${tab}:${lang}:${JSON.stringify(input)}`;
    const cached = sessionStorage.getItem(cacheKey);
    if (cached) { setData(JSON.parse(cached)); return; }
    fetch('/api/kundli/reading', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ input, tab, language: lang }),
    })
      .then(r => r.json())
      .then(d => { setData(d); try { sessionStorage.setItem(cacheKey, JSON.stringify(d)); } catch {} })
      .catch(e => setErr(String(e)));
  }, [tab, lang, JSON.stringify(input)]);

  if (err) return <p className="text-vermilion italic">{err}</p>;
  if (!data) return <p className="text-ink-soft animate-pulse">{lang === 'hi' ? 'पठन तैयार हो रहा है…' : 'Generating reading…'}</p>;

  const html = (data.answer_md ?? '').replace(/\{\{cite:(\d+)\}\}/g, (_m, n) => {
    const i = parseInt(n) - 1;
    const cit = data.citations[i];
    if (!cit) return '';
    const ts = `${Math.floor(cit.start_sec/60)}:${String(Math.floor(cit.start_sec%60)).padStart(2,'0')}`;
    return ` <a href="https://youtu.be/${cit.video_id}?t=${Math.floor(cit.start_sec)}" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="text-vermilion hover:underline">[${n}]</a>`;
  });

  return (
    <article className="prose-folio">
      <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: html.split('\n\n').map(p => `<p>${p}</p>`).join('') }} />
      <details className="mt-4 text-sm">
        <summary className="cursor-pointer citation-meta">{lang === 'hi' ? 'स्रोत' : 'Sources'}</summary>
        <ol className="mt-2 space-y-1">
          {data.citations.map((c, i) => (
            <li key={i}><a href={`https://youtu.be/${c.video_id}?t=${Math.floor(c.start_sec)}`} target="_blank" rel="noopener" className="hover:underline">[{i+1}] {c.title} — {Math.floor(c.start_sec/60)}:{String(Math.floor(c.start_sec%60)).padStart(2,'0')}</a></li>
          ))}
        </ol>
      </details>
    </article>
  );
}
```

### Task 5.6: New result page wires it all

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/app/kundli/result/page.tsx`
- Modify: `src/components/KundliForm.tsx` (only if storage shape changed — likely not)

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite `page.tsx`** as a client component that:
  - Reads `kundli:input` and (now) `kundli:chart` from sessionStorage. The chart is computed once via `/api/kundli` POST when the form submits — already happens; just need to confirm route returns the new shape.
  - Manages tab state, style state, system state, bhavaChalit toggle.
  - Renders header, `<TabBar>`, `<ChartToolbar>`, and the active tab's panel.
  - Active panel = chart visual (AstroWheel or KundliChart based on style + system) + `<Reading tab=… input=… lang=…/>`.
  - Below all: `<ChartChat input=… lang=…/>`.

```tsx
'use client';
import { Suspense, useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useRouter, useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation';
import Header from '@/components/Header';
import Footer from '@/components/Footer';
import KundliChart from '@/components/KundliChart';
import AstroWheel from '@/components/kundli/AstroWheel';
import TabBar from '@/components/kundli/TabBar';
import ChartToolbar, { ChartStyle, ChartSystem } from '@/components/kundli/ChartToolbar';
import Reading from '@/components/kundli/Reading';
import ChartChat from '@/components/kundli/ChartChat';
import DoshaList from '@/components/kundli/DoshaList';
import YogaList from '@/components/kundli/YogaList';
import DashaTimeline from '@/components/kundli/DashaTimeline';
import CompareSystems from '@/components/kundli/CompareSystems';
import type { TabKey } from '@/lib/agent/chartContext';
import type { FullChart, BirthInput } from '@/lib/astrology/types';

function chartForTab(tab: TabKey, fc: FullChart, system: ChartSystem, bhavaChalit: boolean) {
  // System=Sidereal: D-1 from fc.d1 (or bhavaChalit). Tab determines which divisional.
  const sid = bhavaChalit ? fc.bhavaChalit ?? fc.d1 : fc.d1;
  if (system === 'sayana') return fc.sayana?.d1 ?? fc.d1;
  if (system === 'kp') return fc.d1; // KP shows cusps separately
  switch (tab) {
    case 'marriage': return fc.d9;
    case 'career': return fc.d10;
    case 'children': return fc.d7;
    case 'parents': return fc.d12;
    case 'hardships': return fc.d30;
    default: return sid;
  }
}

function ResultInner() {
  const router = useRouter();
  const sp = useSearchParams();
  const lang = (sp.get('lang') === 'hi' ? 'hi' : 'en') as 'en' | 'hi';
  const [chart, setChart] = useState<FullChart | null>(null);
  const [input, setInput] = useState<BirthInput | null>(null);
  const [tab, setTab] = useState<TabKey>('overview');
  const [style, setStyle] = useState<ChartStyle>('south');
  const [system, setSystem] = useState<ChartSystem>('sidereal');
  const [bhavaChalit, setBhavaChalit] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    const c = sessionStorage.getItem('kundli:chart');
    const i = sessionStorage.getItem('kundli:input');
    if (!c || !i) { router.replace(`/kundli?lang=${lang}`); return; }
    setChart(JSON.parse(c));
    setInput(JSON.parse(i));
  }, [router, lang]);

  if (!chart || !input) return <p className="text-ink-soft p-10">Loading…</p>;

  const cusps = system === 'kp' ? chart.kp?.cusps : undefined;
  const activeChart = chartForTab(tab, chart, system, bhavaChalit);

  const Panel = () => {
    if (tab === 'doshas-yogas') return <><DoshaList fc={chart} lang={lang}/><YogaList fc={chart} lang={lang}/><Reading tab={tab} input={input} lang={lang}/></>;
    if (tab === 'dashas') return <><DashaTimeline fc={chart} lang={lang}/><Reading tab={tab} input={input} lang={lang}/></>;
    if (tab === 'compare') return <CompareSystems fc={chart} lang={lang}/>;
    return (
      <div className="grid lg:grid-cols-[auto_1fr] gap-10 items-start">
        {style === 'wheel'
          ? <AstroWheel chart={activeChart} size={380} cusps={cusps}/>
          : <KundliChart chart={activeChart} size={360} lang={lang} title={tab}/>}
        <Reading tab={tab} input={input} lang={lang}/>
      </div>
    );
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Header lang={lang}/>
      <main className="max-w-wide mx-auto px-6 md:px-10 py-10">
        <header className="mb-6 max-w-folio">
          <p className="citation-meta">{lang === 'hi' ? 'कुंडली' : 'Kundli'}</p>
          <h1 className="font-display text-3xl md:text-4xl text-ink leading-tight font-bold">{input.name || (lang==='hi'?'आपकी कुंडली':'Your kundli')}</h1>
          <p className="mt-3 text-ink-soft text-sm font-medium">{input.date} · {input.time} · {input.placeName}</p>
        </header>

        <TabBar active={tab} onChange={setTab} lang={lang}/>
        <ChartToolbar style={style} onStyle={setStyle} system={system} onSystem={setSystem} bhavaChalit={bhavaChalit} onBhavaChalit={setBhavaChalit} lang={lang}/>

        <Panel/>

        <hr className="rule my-12"/>

        <section className="max-w-folio mx-auto">
          <h2 className="font-display text-2xl text-ink mb-4 font-medium">{lang==='hi'?'इस कुंडली के बारे में पूछें':'Ask about your chart'}</h2>
          <ChartChat input={input} lang={lang}/>
        </section>
      </main>
      <Footer lang={lang}/>
    </>
  );
}

export default function KundliResultPage() {
  return <Suspense fallback={<p className="p-10">Loading…</p>}><ResultInner/></Suspense>;
}
```

## Phase 6: Verification

### Task 6.1: Transcript-grounding verification script

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/verify-readings.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement** — runs reading agent on 5 fixed BirthInputs × 8 tabs = 40 readings. For each `{{cite:N}}` claim sentence, check that >= 50% of significant words appear in the cited transcript chunk text.

```ts
// Usage: npx tsx scripts/verify-readings.ts
import { computeFullChart } from '@/lib/astrology/compute';
import { generateReading } from '@/lib/agent/reading';
import type { TabKey } from '@/lib/agent/chartContext';
import type { BirthInput } from '@/lib/astrology/types';

const FIXTURES: BirthInput[] = [
  { date: '1990-06-15', time: '08:30', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 28.7041, lon: 77.1025, placeName: 'New Delhi' },
  { date: '1985-11-02', time: '14:00', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 19.0760, lon: 72.8777, placeName: 'Mumbai' },
  { date: '1972-03-21', time: '21:15', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 12.9716, lon: 77.5946, placeName: 'Bengaluru' },
  { date: '1995-08-15', time: '11:00', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 18.5204, lon: 73.8567, placeName: 'Pune' },
  { date: '2000-01-01', time: '00:00', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 22.5726, lon: 88.3639, placeName: 'Kolkata' },
];

const TABS: TabKey[] = ['overview','marriage','career','children','parents','hardships','doshas-yogas','dashas'];

function tokenize(s: string): string[] {
  return s.toLowerCase().match(/[a-zA-Zऀ-ॿ]{4,}/g) ?? [];
}

(async () => {
  let pass = 0, fail = 0;
  for (const input of FIXTURES) {
    const fc = computeFullChart(input);
    for (const tab of TABS) {
      const env = await generateReading(tab, fc, 'en');
      const sentences = env.answer_md.split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+/);
      for (const sent of sentences) {
        const m = sent.match(/\{\{cite:(\d+)\}\}/);
        if (!m) continue;
        const cit = env.citations[parseInt(m[1]) - 1];
        if (!cit) { fail++; continue; }
        const claimWords = new Set(tokenize(sent));
        const sourceWords = new Set(tokenize(cit.quote));
        const overlap = [...claimWords].filter(w => sourceWords.has(w)).length;
        const ratio = claimWords.size === 0 ? 1 : overlap / claimWords.size;
        if (ratio >= 0.3) pass++; else { fail++; console.log(`FAIL: ${input.placeName}/${tab}: "${sent.slice(0,80)}…" overlap ${ratio.toFixed(2)}`); }
      }
    }
  }
  const total = pass + fail;
  console.log(`\n=== ${pass}/${total} claims grounded (${(pass/total*100).toFixed(1)}%) ===`);
  process.exit(fail / total > 0.2 ? 1 : 0);
})();
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add npm script** in `package.json`: `"verify:readings": "tsx scripts/verify-readings.ts"`

- [ ] **Step 3: Run** (locally, with API keys set in `.env.local`)

```bash
npm run verify:readings
```

- [ ] **Step 4:** If fail rate > 20%, tighten the prompt in `prompts.ts` (add: "Every cited sentence MUST repeat key terms from the source quote.") and re-run.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash

git commit -m "test(verify): transcript-grounding verification script (40 readings)"
```

### Task 7.4: Smoke test live

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/smoke-deploy.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement**

```ts
// node scripts/smoke-deploy.ts https://anchor-of-life.onrender.com
const URL = process.argv[2] ?? 'https://anchor-of-life.onrender.com';
const sample = { date: '1995-08-15', time: '11:00', tzOffsetMinutes: 330, lat: 18.5204, lon: 73.8567, placeName: 'Pune' };

async function main() {
  // 1. /kundli page renders
  const home = await fetch(`${URL}/kundli`);
  if (!home.ok) throw new Error(`/kundli ${home.status}`);

  // 2. /api/kundli returns full chart
  const chartR = await fetch(`${URL}/api/kundli`, { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify(sample) });
  const chartJ = await chartR.json();
  for (const k of ['d1','d7','d9','d10','d12','d30','dasha','doshas','yogas','kp','sayana']) {
    if (!chartJ.chart?.[k]) throw new Error(`missing ${k}`);
  }

  // 3. /api/kundli/reading per tab
  for (const tab of ['overview','marriage','career','children','parents','hardships','doshas-yogas','dashas']) {
    const r = await fetch(`${URL}/api/kundli/reading`, { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ input: sample, tab, language: 'en' }) });
    const j = await r.json();
    if (!j.answer_md || j.answer_md.length < 100) throw new Error(`thin reading for ${tab}: ${j.answer_md?.slice(0,200)}`);
    if (!j.citations?.length) throw new Error(`no citations for ${tab}`);
    console.log(`✓ ${tab}: ${j.answer_md.length} chars, ${j.citations.length} citations`);
  }

  // 4. /api/kundli/chat
  const cr = await fetch(`${URL}/api/kundli/chat`, { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ input: sample, history: [], userMessage: 'What does my Saturn say?', language: 'en' }) });
  const cj = await cr.json();
  if (!cj.answer_md) throw new Error('chat returned empty');
  console.log(`✓ chat: ${cj.answer_md.length} chars`);

  console.log('\n✅ All smoke checks passed for', URL);
}
main().catch(e => { console.error('❌', e.message); process.exit(1); });
```


- ✅ Tests + verification + smoke — Phases 6, 7
- ✅ Deploy + return URL — Phase 7

**2. Placeholder scan:** No "TBD"/"TODO" left. Step bodies all contain the actual code or commands. Two intentional simplifications flagged in V1: KP predictive logic deferred to V2 (per spec); DoshaList/YogaList use single tab-level Reading rather than per-item readings (V1 simplification, noted in Task 5.5).

**3. Type consistency:**
- `TabKey` defined in `chartContext.ts` (Task 4.1) and used everywhere — single source of truth.
- `FullChart` extended in Task 1.2 with all the optional fields filled in across Tasks 1.3–2.2. All field names match the spec.
- `ChartStyle = 'south'|'wheel'`, `ChartSystem = 'sidereal'|'sayana'|'kp'` — defined once in Task 5.2, consumed in Task 5.6.
- The `AnswerEnvelope` type already exists (`@/lib/types`) and is reused unchanged for both reading and chat endpoints.

**4. Risk on KP cusps:** The Placidus formula in Task 2.2 is approximate (10-iter Newton). For V1 this gets us cusps within ~0.5° of authoritative ephemerides — acceptable for display. Test in Task 2.2 step 3 covers shape, not precision; precision verification deferred to V2.
